profile_info.name: Usman Akram
profile_info.bio: Results-oriented Python Developer with 1.5+ years of experience in scalable backend systems, AI automation, and REST APIs using FastAPI. Skilled in web scraping, data mining, and database management. Proven track record with high-performance solutions like a Global Reverse Geocoder.

work_experience.title: Python Developer
work_experience.description: Delivered custom web automation scripts for international clients using Selenium and BeautifulSoup, reducing manual data entry time by over 40%. Developed complex data extraction pipelines to scrape, parse, and clean datasets from dynamic websites for market research. Implemented error-handling protocols in automation scripts to ensure reliability during high-volume data processing.

work_experience.title: Python Developer
work_experience.description: Engineered a high-performance Global Reverse Geocoding API using FastAPI, processing geospatial datasets with 95% accuracy. Developed backend microservices for AI-powered chatbots, optimizing user interaction flows and ensuring 99.9% system uptime. Optimized database queries in MySQL, reducing data retrieval latency for high-traffic applications by 30%

projects.title: AI Model Fine-Tuning & Deployment
projects.description: Fine-tuned pre-trained Large Language Models (LLMs) from Hugging Face on custom datasets to create domain-specific chatbots, reducing response hallucinations by 25%. Built and deployed a deep learning model using TensorFlow to classify unstructured text data, optimized for low-latency inference on edge devices. Developed a containerized AI inference pipeline using Docker, allowing for seamless deployment of models across different server environments.

projects.title: IntraView AI (AI-Based Recruitment System)
projects.description: Architected a comprehensive AI interview system featuring distinct Candidate and Admin panels to automate initial screening. Integrated Hugging Face Transformers (BERT) to implement Natural Language Processing (NLP), enabling the system to analyze candidate sentiment and answer relevance with 85% accuracy. Utilized TensorFlow to train custom neural networks for pattern recognition, enhancing the expert system's decision-making logic for candidate scoring.
projects.technologies: Python, MongoDB, TensorFlow, Hugging Face Transformers, Genetic Algorithms

projects.title: Global Reverse Geocoder
projects.description: Built a robust API capable of converting raw GPS coordinates into human-readable addresses (City, Town, Postal Code) with high precision. Optimized API response times to ensure real-time location processing for mapping applications.

def fizzbuzz(n):
    res=[]
    for i in range(1,n+1):
        s="Fizz"*(i%3==0)+
        "Buzz"*(i%5==0)
        res.append(s or str(i))
        return res

coding_submission.code:
def isValid(s: str) -> bool:
    # Hash map to store the mapping of closing to opening brackets
    mapping = {")": "(", "}": "{", "]": "["}
    stack = []

    for char in s:
        # If the character is a closing bracket
        if char in mapping:
            # Pop the top element from the stack if it's not empty
            # Otherwise, assign a dummy value '#'
            top_element = stack.pop() if stack else '#'

            # Check if the popped opening bracket matches the required mapping
            if mapping[char] != top_element:
                return False
        else:
            # If it's an opening bracket, push it onto the stack
            stack.append(char)

    # If the stack is empty, all brackets were matched correctly
    return not stack

# Test Cases
print(isValid("([])"))    # Output: True
print(isValid("()[]{}"))  # Output: True
print(isValid("(]"))      # Output: False
print(isValid("([)]"))    # Output: False

coding_submission.problem_description: Given two arrays, determine if the second array is a subsequence of the first array. A subsequence is a sequence that can be derived from another sequence by deleting some elements without changing the order of the remaining elements.
coding_submission.code:
def is_subsequence(array, sequence):
    # Initialize pointers for both arrays
    arr_idx = 0
    seq_idx = 0
    
    # Traverse both arrays
    while arr_idx < len(array) and seq_idx < len(sequence):
        # If elements match, move the sequence pointer
        if array[arr_idx] == sequence[seq_idx]:
            seq_idx += 1
        # Always move the main array pointer
        arr_idx += 1
        
    # If we successfully traversed the entire sequence, it's a subsequence
    return seq_idx == len(sequence)

# --- Example Usage ---
main_array = [5, 1, 22, 25, 6, -1, 8, 10]
sub_array = [1, 6, -1, 10]

print(is_subsequence(main_array, sub_array))  # Output: True

invalid_sub = [1, -1, 6]
print(is_subsequence(main_array, invalid_sub))  # Output: False (out of order)
